thesis_status_reasoning: The thesis claims robust structural integrity and sufficient redundancy, but current data shows Falsification Criterion #2 is actively triggered: Pump 3 exhibits 38.7 Hz vibration while operating at 102.4% load (>95%), indicating irreversible cavitation damage is ACTIVELY OCCURRING. With Pump 4 offline and remaining pumps at 99.5-102.4% load (approaching the 115% aggregate threshold), the system has lost its claimed mechanical redundancy. The high reservoir level (95.5%) paradoxically coincides with pumps operating beyond rated capacity, suggesting the distribution mechanism is under extreme stress despite abundant supply.
action_reasoning: Pump 3 must be immediately shut down to prevent catastrophic mechanical failure from active cavitation damage. The system should operate on Pumps 1 and 2 only while emergency repairs are expedited for Pumps 3 and 4, accepting temporarily reduced capacity over imminent total system collapse.

key_signals.signal: Pump 3 vibration at 38.7 Hz with 102.4% load and elevated temperature (92.5°C)
All active pumps operating at 99.5-102.4% of rated capacity with one pump offline
Reservoir level at 95.5% while pumps are severely overloaded
System pressure at 58 PSI remains above 40 PSI regulatory minimum
Supply chain delays preventing Pump 4 repair completion

biggest_uncertainty: Whether Pump 3 has already sustained permanent damage requiring full replacement versus being salvageable if shut down immediately, which determines if the system can restore redundancy or faces prolonged degraded capacity
